title: Daily Briefing — Jun 18
headline: Breadth is wobbly, tech is exhausted, and Energy's rolling over. Flows say: tread carefully.
body: Big picture: We’re seeing a classic late-cycle wobble—SPY (744.54) hanging just below its 20 MA (746.97), with rising VIX (18.44) confirming volatility picking up. Fear & Greed at 32 (Fear), well off last month’s 61.5, but no panic yet. Institutions aren’t panicking—just quietly reducing risk.

Sector rotation is telling: Tech (XLK) remains the strongest but momentum is weakening, with leaders like MSFT (374.67) and META (565.91) well under their 20 MAs, flagging exhaustion. Energy (XLE: 53.33) is breaking down—lagging hard and triggering HIGH sector risk. Materials and Industrials are improving, but every other sector is lagging. Defensive rotation in progress.

Contrarian eyes: Retail is licking wounds (bottom-fishing in ARM, DELL, AMD), but the flows aren’t screaming buy. SPY and QQQ are flirting with 20 MA mean reversion—patience, don’t predict. If bonds freak out, everything breaks, but for now HYG and LQD are holding above their 20 MAs. Keep your finger off the buy trigger until we see capitulation or a clear institutional reversal.
callout_body:
SPY: Watch 746.97 (20 MA)—daily closes below = exit longs
XLK: 186.69 (20 MA) is support, but leaders are weak
XLE: 57.23 (20 MA) broken = avoid
VIX: Above 17.42—volatility rising
HYG/LQD: Both safely above 20 MA—no bond breakdown
Fear & Greed at 32—not extreme, but caution is warranted
action_items: If SPY closes under 746.97 again, reduce equity exposure—rule triggered
Avoid Energy (XLE)—breaking major support and risk is high
closing_quote: If it’s in the press, it’s in the price. Abundance mindset—wait for your A+ setup.
